description_long: This is a small, single-engine, fixed-wing airplane with a sleek, streamlined fuselage and a low-wing configuration. The aircraft is predominantly white with red and gray accent stripes along the side, and the registration 'OK-ATS' is clearly visible on the tail section. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the cockpit features large, clear windows, suggesting good visibility for the pilot and passengers. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel under the front fuselage. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The overall appearance is that of a modern general aviation aircraft, suitable for private or small commercial use.
description: A sleek, white, single-engine low-wing airplane with red and gray stripes, tricycle landing gear, and a conventional tail. The cockpit has large windows and the registration 'OK-ATS' is visible on the tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the left profile, propeller, cockpit windows, wing, and tail with visible registration.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, nose, cockpit windshield, and symmetrical wing layout.
description_view_2: Side view showing the right profile, propeller, cockpit windows, wing, and tail with visible registration.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the trailing edges of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets; slight dihedral angle present.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with large side and front windows, likely accommodating two to four seats.
